If there was a word that was enough to express how much I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ loath this game then I would use it a thousand times over. This game has been utterly neglected by EA to the point where I don't even know how to describe just how bad this game has gotten. I love Star Wars so much and when I first got this game on my PS about a year after it came out I couldn't get enough of it, however returning to this game now with the copy I use on steam I don't recognize it anymore. The only people who still play multiplayer in this game are the lowlifes who have no job live in their mothers basement and don't know what Star Wars is they just love making everyone elses lives miserable. The only modes you can still play in this game are arcade and co-op that's it, and maybe a good multiplayer match during the May 4th season when everyone comes back to this game. The sheer lack of solid level based matchmaking and the terrible EA servers make this game almost unbearable to play, because that one match that you get every thousand matches you play where its actually solid players and everyone is evenly leveled the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game crashes because EA servers had a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ problem! Then every other ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ match is with the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ basement goblins who have zero life and play this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game non stop ruining everyone elses lives. Not to mention that there is still someway somehow hackers in this ancient game, why, why would you waste your time to hack in a game like this its ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ridiculous. Finally, this game is too old and too ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ to have ever become popular and the only reason it did is because its the only good modern Star Wars multiplayer, however since the re-release of the original battlefront II along with its multiplayer returning I don't understand why I ever wanted to play this game again. I know EA won't see my review and even if they did they wouldn't care, but sincerely and with every ounce of my being, ♥♥♥♥ you EA!

It’s incredible how stunning this game looks relative to its modest system requirements. While the online multiplayer player base has dwindled, grabbing it on sale just to play through the single-player campaign is absolutely worth it.
A very fun, but grindy game. If it is for sale go buy it. Servers are pretty dead, and since this is and EA game, of course, EA launcher install is required.

At the current state of the game, I wouldn't recommend purchasing Battlefront 2.
Not that the game is bad when it comes to gameplay or graphics. This is one of my favorites.
My issue is that hackers and annoying glitches are infesting almost EVERY lobby you join. Supremacy? chuds spam in chat about "no lobby will be spared" then mass kill the enemy team (or yours if you're unlucky enough.) Okay, let's try Ewok Hunt. Impossible for Stormtroopers to win because the shuttle arriving animation bugs all player cameras. That's fine. Let's see if Galactic Assault works. Annoying bug/hack where all player cameras are fixed in one spot. The only mode "genre" that actually works as intended is Heroes and Villains, and half the time the players of that mode are straight ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.
This game does not get updates anymore so new player beware youre going up against maxed people in all lobbies that are populated. XD

In theory, this game is incredibly fun. The co-op and galactic assault modes are great fun, and the hero modes can be enjoyable. However, this game cannot be recommended to new players. Especially not the hero modes. You want to play a hero that isn't one of the 5 saber characters on either side? You get called a slur. You want to work with your teammate and play the 2v2 mode like a 2v2 mode? You get called several slurs. Be it players with thousands of hours practicing ways of making any normal duel unwinnable, or people who will immediately turn to verbally attacking you the second you decide to play a blaster hero, this game has one of the worst communities I've ever had the displeasure of experiencing. As much potential I see for fun, and as much as I long for a time passed, this game is not enjoyable. The community is downright awful. At this point, I don't think we deserve a third game.

One of the best team shooters of the modern era; HOWEVER I cannot recommend because EA ended support leaving the game in a completely broken state. Every game has one of three issues: 1. everyone in the match gets their camera locked to a random direction every few minutes which forces everyone to respawn and decides matches by effectively wiping all heroes on the field. 2. No battle points or XP is awarded so everyone can only choose from the 4 base classes (these are the best matches unfortunately). 3. lack of players in general, some modes will have you stuck waiting for hours and require high numbers of players to begin the match. The game is slowly dying and will eventually reach the point where modes like galactic assault and starfighter assault can no longer be played as they cannot be played with bots. EA needs to release a patch fixing all the bugs, it's ridiculous for a game to degrade so rapidly like this when it isn't being actively developed

This game is still the biggest missed oportunnaty to excide original battlefront 2 or at least become battlefront 2.5. At the end it really was abandoned too early.
Multiplayer is one of the key things for this games and has a lot of improvements compered to standard battlefield staff from DICE (namely, galactic assault- operations like game mod, doesnt not force attackers to cap all the points in one sector). It has all the gamemods you require, but my main problems are most of those are locked behind multiplayer, not co-op vs AI mod (that only has galactic assault as its gamemod, nothing else (and here i spent most of the time because i hate playing against other sweaty players) 
Damn shame, game has everything from original battlefront 2 but it does not have full singleplayer support for those multiplayer mods, especiolly galactic conquest.
Other problem for me is that game still lives under the shadow of those pay2win loot boxes. Yes, they no longer exist and everything you either have unlocked or unlock through standard leveling, but all the upgrades (star cards) were really badly designed (which was the point for loot boxes) and both currencis in game are useless.
Oh, and i dont want to talk about campagin. That is most disney slop thing i have ever seen (on same level as bad as Kenobi and Ansoka series). Even tho orignal is better, it still aged poorly for me considering how much better clone wars are(yes, Feloni is as much of an heck as rest of the lucasfilm, at least that was the only good thing he produced and to be honest, original EU clone wars were not good)

This is going to be my first review on Steam. I have played many games, but this is my first review. Just a disclaimer: I am a campaign guy, so I have only played the campaign part of this game, but I have some thoughts.
I have played a lot of campaign games over the years, but this game is not really a campaign game in my opinion. It's more gameplay than story, a ratio of about 90% gameplay and 10% story. You just keep fighting and killing troopers; that's mostly what goes on. On top of that, the story and gameplay don't give you enough time to sympathize with the characters or recognize their emotions. It doesn't let you soak in whatever is going on between the lines. However, the game handles the fan service part very nicely. You can play as multiple iconic characters, like Han Solo.

Coming to the verdict, I'll give this game a B-.

There are some issues. The character sometimes starts lagging, and there's a bug where the audio sounds weird if you are using your earphones' mic. The game is also not fully optimized. On top of that, you have to download and log in to the EA launcher to play the game, but that's not a bug, that's a curse.

As for the campaign, it only took me 7 hours to complete, and I'm not even that good at the game when it comes to flying X-wings. It took me several tries on some sections, yet I still finished it in seven hours. For only a seven-hour campaign, it can feel very grindy at times.

Battlefront 2 is a must play game for any fan of shooters or Star Wars. The gunplay feels fluid and smooth, with you being able to switch between third or first person camera angles. Lightsaber combat is fun but I personally haven't gotten the hang of it yet. The game is still quite active in some modes like supremecy but others are a bit more neglected. It's a shame that DICE abandoned this game to work on Battlefield and there isn't a Battlefront 3. If you want more content KYBER is a good source of it. It's run by fans and has a server browser along with new heroes being added to the game.

500 hours on Xbox.

Actual masterpiece of a video game that EA left to rot and die during it's hayday. Instead of making a Battlefront III like everyone has wanted for YEARS, EA pulled the plug for DICE to make Battlefield 2042.

My only complaint is that there are rarely updates and when there is it is usually for bug testing yet I am glad they are fixing the bugs and keeping this game alive for longer.

Star Wars Battlefront II is a great game because it combines exciting action, impressive graphics, and iconic characters from the Star Wars universe. Players can fight in large battles across famous planets like Hoth, Endor, and Naboo while using heroes and villains such as Darth Vader, Luke Skywalker, and Rey. The game offers a variety of modes, including multiplayer battles, co-op missions, and a story campaign, which keeps the gameplay fun and interesting. Its detailed environments, realistic sound effects, and cinematic feel make players feel like they are truly part of the Star Wars galaxy.
